output.notes: The online postcode lookup system is not working as the PDF schedule is not found. The website states that food waste goes in the brown bin, recyclable waste in the blue/green bin, and non-recyclable waste in the black bin. Garden waste should not go in the black bin. For further information, residents are advised to call 0330 137 4047 or email environment@nmandd.org.

parts.content: You are a helpful web-browsing agent who researches and retrieves bin collection information for a specific council area by accessing their official website and lookup system

### Bin Collection Schedule Lookup Task

## Objective

Research and retrieve bin collection information for a specific council area by accessing their official website and lookup system.

## Instructions

# Step 1: Navigate to Council Website
Access the council website at: http://www.newrymournedown.org/bin-collection-information-and-calendar

# Step 2: Test Postcodes
Search for the collection schedules for this postcodes: BT24 7AF
If a full address is required, use any house number (e.g., "1 [Street Name]") or select any option on a dropdown. The specific address doesn't matter - we only need the collection schedule format

## Output

Provide the following information for each bin type (if exists) + the postcode:
    next_pickup_day
    frequency 
    bin_colour

If it doesn't exist, that is ok. Just answer None for these fields.

## Troubleshooting
If you encounter issues:

Look for alternative entry points (e.g., "Check my bin day")
Note any technical difficulties in your response
If the provided postcode doesn't work try postcodes from the same council area (use your knowledge of local postcodes).

### Ran Playwright code
```js
// Navigate to http://www.newrymournedown.org/bin-collection-information-and-calendar
await page.goto('http://www.newrymournedown.org/bin-collection-information-and-calendar');
```

      - heading "We aim to collect all bins on their scheduled collection day. However, sometimes for various reasons we may miss some. When a delay has occurred we will return to collect your bin on the next available day. We are asking all households to do the following:" [level=2] [ref=e1312]
      - list [ref=e1313]:

          - text: Place bins out for collection no later than
          - strong [ref=e1315]: 6am
          - text: every morning or alternatively the night before.
        - listitem [ref=e1316]: Please do not put out extra bins or place additional rubbish bags next to your bin as we cannot facilitate the collection of these.
        - listitem [ref=e1317]: Help to keep our schedules on track by being mindful of where you park, making sure your vehicle doesn’t limit access on bin collection days – and spread the word to your neighbours too – thank you!

          - strong [ref=e1320]: NOT

          - strong [ref=e1315]: 6am
          - text: every morning or alternatively the night before.
        - listitem [ref=e1316]: Please do not put out extra bins or place additional rubbish bags next to your bin as we cannot facilitate the collection of these.
        - listitem [ref=e1317]: Help to keep our schedules on track by being mindful of where you park, making sure your vehicle doesn’t limit access on bin collection days – and spread the word to your neighbours too – thank you!
        - listitem [ref=e1318]: Residents are encouraged to maximise the use of their household bins by using the correct bin for your waste items – ie Black bin, Brown bin and Blue bin.
        - listitem [ref=e1319]:
          - text: Remember Food or Recyclable Waste should
          - strong [ref=e1320]: NOT
          - text: be placed in your Black Bin. Bins containing the wrong materials may not be collected.
      - paragraph [ref=e1321]:
        - strong [ref=e1322]: "TIPS:"
      - list [ref=e1323]:
        - listitem [ref=e1324]:
          - strong [ref=e1325]: "BLUE:"
          - text: Flatten boxes
        - listitem [ref=e1326]:
          - strong [ref=e1327]: "BLUE:"
          - text: Flatten containers and remove lids
        - listitem [ref=e1328]:
          - strong [ref=e1329]: "BLACK:"
          - text: Do not place garden waste into your black bin
        - listitem [ref=e1330]:
          - strong [ref=e1331]: "BROWN:"
          - text: Use this bin for all food waste, start composting your garden waste
        - listitem [ref=e1332]: Wash your hands when handling bins and ensure bin handles are cleaned or wiped-down before and after use. Our staff will appreciate this
        - listitem [ref=e1333]: Do not approach our staff without following the minimum two-metre social distancing guidance.
        - listitem [ref=e1334]: You must not put items such as used tissues, gloves or disposable cleaning cloths into your recycling bins. This is very important, they must be bagged and placed into your black bin.
      - heading "Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!" [level=2] [ref=e1335]:

    - generic [ref=e1308]:
      - heading "Weekly Bin Collections and Calendar" [level=1] [ref=e1309]
      - 'heading "Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!" [level=3] [ref=e1310]':
        - generic [ref=e1311]: "Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!"
      - heading "We aim to collect all bins on their scheduled collection day. However, sometimes for various reasons we may miss some. When a delay has occurred we will return to collect your bin on the next available day. We are asking all households to do the following:" [level=2] [ref=e1312]
      - list [ref=e1313]:
        - listitem [ref=e1314]:
          - text: Place bins out for collection no later than
          - strong [ref=e1315]: 6am
          - text: every morning or alternatively the night before.
        - listitem [ref=e1316]: Please do not put out extra bins or place additional rubbish bags next to your bin as we cannot facilitate the collection of these.
        - listitem [ref=e1317]: Help to keep our schedules on track by being mindful of where you park, making sure your vehicle doesn’t limit access on bin collection days – and spread the word to your neighbours too – thank you!
        - listitem [ref=e1318]: Residents are encouraged to maximise the use of their household bins by using the correct bin for your waste items – ie Black bin, Brown bin and Blue bin.
        - listitem [ref=e1319]:
          - text: Remember Food or Recyclable Waste should
          - strong [ref=e1320]: NOT
          - text: be placed in your Black Bin. Bins containing the wrong materials may not be collected.
      - paragraph [ref=e1321]:
        - strong [ref=e1322]: "TIPS:"
      - list [ref=e1323]:
        - listitem [ref=e1324]:
          - strong [ref=e1325]: "BLUE:"
          - text: Flatten boxes
        - listitem [ref=e1326]:
          - strong [ref=e1327]: "BLUE:"
          - text: Flatten containers and remove lids
        - listitem [ref=e1328]:
          - strong [ref=e1329]: "BLACK:"
          - text: Do not place garden waste into your black bin
        - listitem [ref=e1330]:
          - strong [ref=e1331]: "BROWN:"
          - text: Use this bin for all food waste, start composting your garden waste
        - listitem [ref=e1332]: Wash your hands when handling bins and ensure bin handles are cleaned or wiped-down before and after use. Our staff will appreciate this
        - listitem [ref=e1333]: Do not approach our staff without following the minimum two-metre social distancing guidance.
        - listitem [ref=e1334]: You must not put items such as used tissues, gloves or disposable cleaning cloths into your recycling bins. This is very important, they must be bagged and placed into your black bin.
      - heading "Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!" [level=2] [ref=e1335]:
        - strong [ref=e1336]: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!
      - heading "To Access Your Weekly Bin Collection Schedule:" [level=3] [ref=e1337]
      - paragraph [ref=e1338]:
        - text: 1. Under '
        - strong [ref=e1339]: SELECT
        - text: "' move the arrow to the correct"
        - strong [ref=e1340]: FIRST 4 DIGITS
        - text: of your postcode and click your mouse to select it e.g BT24
      - paragraph [ref=e1341]:
        - text: 2. Move across to the second box and enter the
        - strong [ref=e1342]: LAST 3 digits
        - text: of your postcode e.g. 8ZH
      - paragraph [ref=e1343]:
        - text: 3. Finally click on '
        - strong [ref=e1344]: SEARCH
        - text: "' to view your 'Weekly Bin Collection Schedule'"
      - paragraph [ref=e1347]:
        - text: "Enter your postcode to view bin collection dates for you area:"
        - generic [ref=e1349]: (you will need to select the first part of your postcode where it says 'Select' before you begin)
      - generic [ref=e1350]:

    - generic [ref=e1308]:
      - heading "Weekly Bin Collections and Calendar" [level=1] [ref=e1309]
      - 'heading "Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!" [level=3] [ref=e1310]':
        - generic [ref=e1311]: "Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!"
      - heading "We aim to collect all bins on their scheduled collection day. However, sometimes for various reasons we may miss some. When a delay has occurred we will return to collect your bin on the next available day. We are asking all households to do the following:" [level=2] [ref=e1312]
      - list [ref=e1313]:
        - listitem [ref=e1314]:
          - text: Place bins out for collection no later than
          - strong [ref=e1315]: 6am
          - text: every morning or alternatively the night before.
        - listitem [ref=e1316]: Please do not put out extra bins or place additional rubbish bags next to your bin as we cannot facilitate the collection of these.
        - listitem [ref=e1317]: Help to keep our schedules on track by being mindful of where you park, making sure your vehicle doesn’t limit access on bin collection days – and spread the word to your neighbours too – thank you!
        - listitem [ref=e1318]: Residents are encouraged to maximise the use of their household bins by using the correct bin for your waste items – ie Black bin, Brown bin and Blue bin.
        - listitem [ref=e1319]:
          - text: Remember Food or Recyclable Waste should
          - strong [ref=e1320]: NOT
          - text: be placed in your Black Bin. Bins containing the wrong materials may not be collected.
      - paragraph [ref=e1321]:
        - strong [ref=e1322]: "TIPS:"
      - list [ref=e1323]:
        - listitem [ref=e1324]:
          - strong [ref=e1325]: "BLUE:"
          - text: Flatten boxes
        - listitem [ref=e1326]:
          - strong [ref=e1327]: "BLUE:"
          - text: Flatten containers and remove lids
        - listitem [ref=e1328]:
          - strong [ref=e1329]: "BLACK:"
          - text: Do not place garden waste into your black bin
        - listitem [ref=e1330]:
          - strong [ref=e1331]: "BROWN:"
          - text: Use this bin for all food waste, start composting your garden waste
        - listitem [ref=e1332]: Wash your hands when handling bins and ensure bin handles are cleaned or wiped-down before and after use. Our staff will appreciate this
        - listitem [ref=e1333]: Do not approach our staff without following the minimum two-metre social distancing guidance.
        - listitem [ref=e1334]: You must not put items such as used tissues, gloves or disposable cleaning cloths into your recycling bins. This is very important, they must be bagged and placed into your black bin.
      - heading "Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!" [level=2] [ref=e1335]:
        - strong [ref=e1336]: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!
      - heading "To Access Your Weekly Bin Collection Schedule:" [level=3] [ref=e1337]
      - paragraph [ref=e1338]:
        - text: 1. Under '
        - strong [ref=e1339]: SELECT
        - text: "' move the arrow to the correct"
        - strong [ref=e1340]: FIRST 4 DIGITS
        - text: of your postcode and click your mouse to select it e.g BT24
      - paragraph [ref=e1341]:
        - text: 2. Move across to the second box and enter the
        - strong [ref=e1342]: LAST 3 digits
        - text: of your postcode e.g. 8ZH
      - paragraph [ref=e1343]:
        - text: 3. Finally click on '
        - strong [ref=e1344]: SEARCH
        - text: "' to view your 'Weekly Bin Collection Schedule'"
      - paragraph [ref=e1347]:
        - text: "Enter your postcode to view bin collection dates for you area:"
        - generic [ref=e1349]: (you will need to select the first part of your postcode where it says 'Select' before you begin)

    - generic [ref=e1308]:
      - heading "Weekly Bin Collections and Calendar" [level=1] [ref=e1309]
      - 'heading "Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!" [level=3] [ref=e1310]':
        - generic [ref=e1311]: "Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!"
      - heading "We aim to collect all bins on their scheduled collection day. However, sometimes for various reasons we may miss some. When a delay has occurred we will return to collect your bin on the next available day. We are asking all households to do the following:" [level=2] [ref=e1312]
      - list [ref=e1313]:
        - listitem [ref=e1314]:
          - text: Place bins out for collection no later than
          - strong [ref=e1315]: 6am
          - text: every morning or alternatively the night before.
        - listitem [ref=e1316]: Please do not put out extra bins or place additional rubbish bags next to your bin as we cannot facilitate the collection of these.
        - listitem [ref=e1317]: Help to keep our schedules on track by being mindful of where you park, making sure your vehicle doesn’t limit access on bin collection days – and spread the word to your neighbours too – thank you!
        - listitem [ref=e1318]: Residents are encouraged to maximise the use of their household bins by using the correct bin for your waste items – ie Black bin, Brown bin and Blue bin.
        - listitem [ref=e1319]:
          - text: Remember Food or Recyclable Waste should
          - strong [ref=e1320]: NOT
          - text: be placed in your Black Bin. Bins containing the wrong materials may not be collected.
      - paragraph [ref=e1321]:
        - strong [ref=e1322]: "TIPS:"
      - list [ref=e1323]:
        - listitem [ref=e1324]:
          - strong [ref=e1325]: "BLUE:"
          - text: Flatten boxes
        - listitem [ref=e1326]:
          - strong [ref=e1327]: "BLUE:"
          - text: Flatten containers and remove lids
        - listitem [ref=e1328]:
          - strong [ref=e1329]: "BLACK:"
          - text: Do not place garden waste into your black bin
        - listitem [ref=e1330]:
          - strong [ref=e1331]: "BROWN:"
          - text: Use this bin for all food waste, start composting your garden waste
        - listitem [ref=e1332]: Wash your hands when handling bins and ensure bin handles are cleaned or wiped-down before and after use. Our staff will appreciate this
        - listitem [ref=e1333]: Do not approach our staff without following the minimum two-metre social distancing guidance.
        - listitem [ref=e1334]: You must not put items such as used tissues, gloves or disposable cleaning cloths into your recycling bins. This is very important, they must be bagged and placed into your black bin.
      - heading "Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!" [level=2] [ref=e1335]:
        - strong [ref=e1336]: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!
      - heading "To Access Your Weekly Bin Collection Schedule:" [level=3] [ref=e1337]
      - paragraph [ref=e1338]:
        - text: 1. Under '
        - strong [ref=e1339]: SELECT
        - text: "' move the arrow to the correct"
        - strong [ref=e1340]: FIRST 4 DIGITS
        - text: of your postcode and click your mouse to select it e.g BT24
      - paragraph [ref=e1341]:
        - text: 2. Move across to the second box and enter the
        - strong [ref=e1342]: LAST 3 digits
        - text: of your postcode e.g. 8ZH
      - paragraph [ref=e1343]:

Weekly Bin Collections and Calendar
Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!
We aim to collect all bins on their scheduled collection day. However, sometimes for various reasons we may miss some. When a delay has occurred we will return to collect your bin on the next available day. We are asking all households to do the following:
Place bins out for collection no later than 6am every morning or alternatively the night before.
Please do not put out extra bins or place additional rubbish bags next to your bin as we cannot facilitate the collection of these.
Help to keep our schedules on track by being mindful of where you park, making sure your vehicle doesn’t limit access on bin collection days – and spread the word to your neighbours too – thank you! 
Residents are encouraged to maximise the use of their household bins by using the correct bin for your waste items – ie Black bin, Brown bin and Blue bin.
Remember Food or Recyclable Waste should NOT be placed in your Black Bin. Bins containing the wrong materials may not be collected.

TIPS:

BLUE: Flatten boxes
BLUE: Flatten containers and remove lids
BLACK: Do not place garden waste into your black bin
BROWN: Use this bin for all food waste, start composting your garden waste 
Wash your hands when handling bins and ensure bin handles are cleaned or wiped-down before and after use. Our staff will appreciate this
Do not approach our staff without following the minimum two-metre social distancing guidance.
You must not put items such as used tissues, gloves or disposable cleaning cloths into your recycling bins. This is very important, they must be bagged and placed into your black bin.
Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!
To Access Your Weekly Bin Collection Schedule:

1. Under  'SELECT' move the arrow to the correct FIRST 4 DIGITS of your postcode and click your mouse to select it e.g BT24

2. Move across to the second box and enter the LAST 3 digits of your postcode e.g. 8ZH

3. Finally click on 'SEARCH' to view your 'Weekly Bin Collection Schedule'

Enter your postcode to view bin collection dates for you area:
(you will need to select the first part of your postcode where it says 'Select' before you begin)

Weekly Bin Collections and Calendar
Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!
We aim to collect all bins on their scheduled collection day. However, sometimes for various reasons we may miss some. When a delay has occurred we will return to collect your bin on the next available day. We are asking all households to do the following:
Place bins out for collection no later than 6am every morning or alternatively the night before.
Please do not put out extra bins or place additional rubbish bags next to your bin as we cannot facilitate the collection of these.
Help to keep our schedules on track by being mindful of where you park, making sure your vehicle doesn’t limit access on bin collection days – and spread the word to your neighbours too – thank you! 
Residents are encouraged to maximise the use of their household bins by using the correct bin for your waste items – ie Black bin, Brown bin and Blue bin.
Remember Food or Recyclable Waste should NOT be placed in your Black Bin. Bins containing the wrong materials may not be collected.

TIPS:

BLUE: Flatten boxes
BLUE: Flatten containers and remove lids
BLACK: Do not place garden waste into your black bin
BROWN: Use this bin for all food waste, start composting your garden waste 
Wash your hands when handling bins and ensure bin handles are cleaned or wiped-down before and after use. Our staff will appreciate this
Do not approach our staff without following the minimum two-metre social distancing guidance.
You must not put items such as used tissues, gloves or disposable cleaning cloths into your recycling bins. This is very important, they must be bagged and placed into your black bin.
Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!
To Access Your Weekly Bin Collection Schedule:

1. Under  'SELECT' move the arrow to the correct FIRST 4 DIGITS of your postcode and click your mouse to select it e.g BT24

2. Move across to the second box and enter the LAST 3 digits of your postcode e.g. 8ZH

3. Finally click on 'SEARCH' to view your 'Weekly Bin Collection Schedule'

Enter your postcode to view bin collection dates for you area:
(you will need to select the first part of your postcode where it says 'Select' before you begin)

Weekly Bin Collections and Calendar
Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!
We aim to collect all bins on their scheduled collection day. However, sometimes for various reasons we may miss some. When a delay has occurred we will return to collect your bin on the next available day. We are asking all households to do the following:
Place bins out for collection no later than 6am every morning or alternatively the night before.
Please do not put out extra bins or place additional rubbish bags next to your bin as we cannot facilitate the collection of these.
Help to keep our schedules on track by being mindful of where you park, making sure your vehicle doesn’t limit access on bin collection days – and spread the word to your neighbours too – thank you! 
Residents are encouraged to maximise the use of their household bins by using the correct bin for your waste items – ie Black bin, Brown bin and Blue bin.
Remember Food or Recyclable Waste should NOT be placed in your Black Bin. Bins containing the wrong materials may not be collected.

TIPS:

BLUE: Flatten boxes
BLUE: Flatten containers and remove lids
BLACK: Do not place garden waste into your black bin
BROWN: Use this bin for all food waste, start composting your garden waste 
Wash your hands when handling bins and ensure bin handles are cleaned or wiped-down before and after use. Our staff will appreciate this
Do not approach our staff without following the minimum two-metre social distancing guidance.
You must not put items such as used tissues, gloves or disposable cleaning cloths into your recycling bins. This is very important, they must be bagged and placed into your black bin.
Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!
To Access Your Weekly Bin Collection Schedule:

1. Under  'SELECT' move the arrow to the correct FIRST 4 DIGITS of your postcode and click your mouse to select it e.g BT24

2. Move across to the second box and enter the LAST 3 digits of your postcode e.g. 8ZH

3. Finally click on 'SEARCH' to view your 'Weekly Bin Collection Schedule'

Enter your postcode to view bin collection dates for you area:
(you will need to select the first part of your postcode where it says 'Select' before you begin)

Weekly Bin Collections and Calendar
Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!
We aim to collect all bins on their scheduled collection day. However, sometimes for various reasons we may miss some. When a delay has occurred we will return to collect your bin on the next available day. We are asking all households to do the following:
Place bins out for collection no later than 6am every morning or alternatively the night before.
Please do not put out extra bins or place additional rubbish bags next to your bin as we cannot facilitate the collection of these.
Help to keep our schedules on track by being mindful of where you park, making sure your vehicle doesn’t limit access on bin collection days – and spread the word to your neighbours too – thank you! 
Residents are encouraged to maximise the use of their household bins by using the correct bin for your waste items – ie Black bin, Brown bin and Blue bin.
Remember Food or Recyclable Waste should NOT be placed in your Black Bin. Bins containing the wrong materials may not be collected.

TIPS:

BLUE: Flatten boxes
BLUE: Flatten containers and remove lids
BLACK: Do not place garden waste into your black bin
BROWN: Use this bin for all food waste, start composting your garden waste 
Wash your hands when handling bins and ensure bin handles are cleaned or wiped-down before and after use. Our staff will appreciate this
Do not approach our staff without following the minimum two-metre social distancing guidance.
You must not put items such as used tissues, gloves or disposable cleaning cloths into your recycling bins. This is very important, they must be bagged and placed into your black bin.
Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!
To Access Your Weekly Bin Collection Schedule:

1. Under  'SELECT' move the arrow to the correct FIRST 4 DIGITS of your postcode and click your mouse to select it e.g BT24

2. Move across to the second box and enter the LAST 3 digits of your postcode e.g. 8ZH

3. Finally click on 'SEARCH' to view your 'Weekly Bin Collection Schedule'

Enter your postcode to view bin collection dates for you area:
(you will need to select the first part of your postcode where it says 'Select' before you begin)

Weekly Bin Collections and Calendar
Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!
We aim to collect all bins on their scheduled collection day. However, sometimes for various reasons we may miss some. When a delay has occurred we will return to collect your bin on the next available day. We are asking all households to do the following:
Place bins out for collection no later than 6am every morning or alternatively the night before.
Please do not put out extra bins or place additional rubbish bags next to your bin as we cannot facilitate the collection of these.
Help to keep our schedules on track by being mindful of where you park, making sure your vehicle doesn’t limit access on bin collection days – and spread the word to your neighbours too – thank you! 
Residents are encouraged to maximise the use of their household bins by using the correct bin for your waste items – ie Black bin, Brown bin and Blue bin.
Remember Food or Recyclable Waste should NOT be placed in your Black Bin. Bins containing the wrong materials may not be collected.

TIPS:

BLUE: Flatten boxes
BLUE: Flatten containers and remove lids
BLACK: Do not place garden waste into your black bin
BROWN: Use this bin for all food waste, start composting your garden waste 
Wash your hands when handling bins and ensure bin handles are cleaned or wiped-down before and after use. Our staff will appreciate this
Do not approach our staff without following the minimum two-metre social distancing guidance.
You must not put items such as used tissues, gloves or disposable cleaning cloths into your recycling bins. This is very important, they must be bagged and placed into your black bin.
Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!
To Access Your Weekly Bin Collection Schedule:

1. Under  'SELECT' move the arrow to the correct FIRST 4 DIGITS of your postcode and click your mouse to select it e.g BT24

2. Move across to the second box and enter the LAST 3 digits of your postcode e.g. 8ZH

3. Finally click on 'SEARCH' to view your 'Weekly Bin Collection Schedule'

Enter your postcode to view bin collection dates for you area:
(you will need to select the first part of your postcode where it says 'Select' before you begin)

Weekly Bin Collections and Calendar
Please Note: Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Are Now Available for Download on this page-see below!
We aim to collect all bins on their scheduled collection day. However, sometimes for various reasons we may miss some. When a delay has occurred we will return to collect your bin on the next available day. We are asking all households to do the following:
Place bins out for collection no later than 6am every morning or alternatively the night before.
Please do not put out extra bins or place additional rubbish bags next to your bin as we cannot facilitate the collection of these.
Help to keep our schedules on track by being mindful of where you park, making sure your vehicle doesn’t limit access on bin collection days – and spread the word to your neighbours too – thank you! 
Residents are encouraged to maximise the use of their household bins by using the correct bin for your waste items – ie Black bin, Brown bin and Blue bin.
Remember Food or Recyclable Waste should NOT be placed in your Black Bin. Bins containing the wrong materials may not be collected.

TIPS:

BLUE: Flatten boxes
BLUE: Flatten containers and remove lids
BLACK: Do not place garden waste into your black bin
BROWN: Use this bin for all food waste, start composting your garden waste 
Wash your hands when handling bins and ensure bin handles are cleaned or wiped-down before and after use. Our staff will appreciate this
Do not approach our staff without following the minimum two-metre social distancing guidance.
You must not put items such as used tissues, gloves or disposable cleaning cloths into your recycling bins. This is very important, they must be bagged and placed into your black bin.
Updated 2025/26 Bin Collection Calendars Now Available!
To Access Your Weekly Bin Collection Schedule:

1. Under  'SELECT' move the arrow to the correct FIRST 4 DIGITS of your postcode and click your mouse to select it e.g BT24

2. Move across to the second box and enter the LAST 3 digits of your postcode e.g. 8ZH

3. Finally click on 'SEARCH' to view your 'Weekly Bin Collection Schedule'

Enter your postcode to view bin collection dates for you area:
(you will need to select the first part of your postcode where it says 'Select' before you begin)

      - paragraph [ref=e1331]:
        - text: Please place your bin at the kerbside no later than
        - strong [ref=e1332]: 6am
        - text: . Please leave the bin with handles facing the road and the lid closed. We will not collect loose or bagged waste.
      - paragraph [ref=e1333]:
        - strong [ref=e1334]: IN THE EVENT OF UNFORESEEN CIRCUMSTANCES (E.G ADVERSE WEATHER) AND YOUR BIN HAS NOT BEEN COLLECTED PLEASE LEAVE YOUR BIN AT THE KERBSIDE FOR CATCH UP COLLECTION SERVICES.
      - paragraph [ref=e1335]:
        - strong [ref=e1336]:
          - generic [ref=e1337]: Brown Bin
          - text: ":"
        - text: food waste must go in the brown bin. You can purchase compostable brown bin liners in most local shops and supermarkets. Old newspapers can also be used to line your brown bin.
        - strong [ref=e1338]: Strictly no food waste in the black bin.
      - paragraph [ref=e1339]:
        - strong [ref=e1340]:
          - generic [ref=e1341]: Blue Bin
          - text: ":"
        - text: "all clean and dry recyclable waste including glass bottles and jars should go in the blue bin. Materials that can be recycled in your blue bin are:"
      - list [ref=e1342]:
        - listitem [ref=e1343]: mixed paper, newspaper and magazines
        - listitem [ref=e1344]: cardboard
        - listitem [ref=e1345]: metal cans and aerosols

      - paragraph [ref=e1349]:
        - text: For more detailed information about what you can put in your brown/ blue bin

      - heading "Battery Recycling" [level=3] [ref=e1351]
      - paragraph [ref=e1352]: Batteries must not be placed in the Council’s black, brown or blue/green wheelie bins. This is because batteries contain dangerous chemicals. When batteries are mixed with other waste they can cause fires at waste and recycling facilities, causing damage and posing a danger to those who work there.
      - paragraph [ref=e1353]:
        - strong [ref=e1354]: So where can you safely recycle batteries?
      - paragraph [ref=e1355]:
        - text: Batteries can be recycled at your local
        - strong [ref=e1356]: Household Recycling Centre (HRC)
        - text: where there are bins clearly labelled for batteries.
      - paragraph [ref=e1357]: Some shops and supermarkets also provide recycling bins for batteries in store and your local school or community group may also have set up a collection scheme.
      - paragraph [ref=e1358]: "Remember the following when recycling batteries:"
      - list [ref=e1359]:
        - listitem [ref=e1360]: never put batteries in normal rubbish or recycle bins
        - listitem [ref=e1361]: only recycle batteries at your local Household Recycling Site or by using a proper battery recycling and collection scheme
        - listitem [ref=e1362]: remove batteries from devices if you can and recycle both the battery and the device separately in the bins provided at your local Household Recycling Centre
        - listitem [ref=e1363]: if you can’t remove the battery from the device recycle both together in the small electrical items bin at the HRC.
      - paragraph [ref=e1364]:
        - text: Still unsure what to do visit
        - link "www.takecharge.co.uk" [ref=e1365] [cursor=pointer]:

        - text: or email
        - link "environment@nmandd.org" [ref=e1366] [cursor=pointer]:
          - /url: "mailto:environment@nmandd.org "

      - paragraph [ref=e1369]: Refuse crews must report if they damage a bin whilst emptying. If this happens, the Council will replace your bin.
      - paragraph [ref=e1370]:

args.notes: The online postcode lookup system is not working as the PDF schedule is not found. The website states that food waste goes in the brown bin, recyclable waste in the blue/green bin, and non-recyclable waste in the black bin. Garden waste should not go in the black bin. For further information, residents are advised to call 0330 137 4047 or email environment@nmandd.org.
